# pyModbusTCP {{ badge_tests }} {{ badge_docs }}

A simple Modbus/TCP client library for Python.
pyModbusTCP is pure Python code without any extension or external module dependency.

Since version 0.1.0, a server is also available.

## Tests

The module is currently test on Python 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11 and 3.12.

For Linux, Mac OS and Windows.

## Documentation

Documentation of the last release is available online at <https://pymodbustcp.readthedocs.io/>.

## Setup

You can install this package from:

PyPI, the easy way:

```bash
# install the last available release (stable)
sudo pip install pyModbusTCP
```

```bash
# install a specific version (here release v0.1.10)
sudo pip install pyModbusTCP==v0.1.10
```

From GitHub:

```bash
# install a specific version (here release v0.1.10) directly from github servers
sudo pip install git+https://github.com/sourceperl/pyModbusTCP.git@v0.1.10
```

Note on the use of versions:

Over time, some things can change. So, it's a good practice that you always use a specific version of a package for
your project, instead of just relying on the default behavior. Without precision, the installation tools will always
install the latest version available for a package, this may have some drawbacks. For example, in pyModbusTCP, the TCP
automatic open mode will be active by default from version 0.2.0. It is not the case with previous versions and it just
doesn't exist before the 0.0.12. This can lead to some strange behaviour of your application if you are not aware of
the change. Look at [CHANGES](https://github.com/sourceperl/pyModbusTCP/blob/master/CHANGES) for details on versions
available.

## Usage example

See examples/ for full scripts.

### include (for all samples)

```python
from pyModbusTCP.client import ModbusClient
```

### module init (TCP always open)

```python
# TCP auto connect on first modbus request
c = ModbusClient(host="localhost", port=502, unit_id=1, auto_open=True)
```

### module init (TCP open/close for each request)

```python
# TCP auto connect on modbus request, close after it
c = ModbusClient(host="127.0.0.1", auto_open=True, auto_close=True)
```

### Read 2x 16 bits registers at modbus address 0 :

```python
regs = c.read_holding_registers(0, 2)

if regs:
    print(regs)
else:
    print("read error")
```

### Write value 44 and 55 to registers at modbus address 10 :

```python
if c.write_multiple_registers(10, [44,55]):
    print("write ok")
else:
    print("write error")
```

functions.name: pyModbusTCP.utils.get_bits_from_int
documentation:
Get the list of bits of val_int integer (default size is 16 bits).

Return bits list, the least significant bit first. Use list.reverse() for msb first.

```
param val_int

integer value

type val_int

int

param val_size

bit length of integer (word = 16, long = 32) (optional)

type val_size

int

returns

list of boolean "bits" (the least significant first)

rtype

list
```
bits function

```
int

returns

the number of bytes

rtype

int
```

functions.name: pyModbusTCP.utils.word_list_to_long
documentation:
Word list (16 bits) to long (32 bits) or long long (64 bits) list.

By default, word_list_to_long() use big endian order. For use little endian, set
big_endian param to False. Output format could be long long with long_long.
option set to True.

```
param val_list

list of 16 bits int value

type val_list

list

param big_endian

True for big endian/False for little (optional)

type big_endian

bool

param long_long

True for long long 64 bits, default is long 32 bits (optional)

type long_long

bool

returns

list of 32 bits int value

rtype

list
```
Word convert functions

functions.name: pyModbusTCP.utils.long_list_to_word
documentation:
Long (32 bits) or long long (64 bits) list to word (16 bits) list.

By default long_list_to_word() use big endian order. For use little endian, set
big_endian param to False. Input format could be long long with long_long
param to True.

```
param val_list

list of 32 bits int value

type val_list

list

param big_endian

True for big endian/False for little (optional)

type big_endian

bool

param long_long

True for long long 64 bits, default is long 32 bits (optional)

type long_long

bool

returns

list of 16 bits int value

rtype

list
```

functions.name: pyModbusTCP.utils.decode_ieee
documentation:
Decode Python int (32 bits integer) as an IEEE single or double precision format.

Support NaN.

```
param val_int

a 32 or 64 bits integer as an int Python value

type val_int

int

param double

set to decode as a 64 bits double precision,
default is 32 bits single (optional)

type double

bool

returns

float result

rtype

float
```
IEEE floating-point functions

functions.name: pyModbusTCP.utils.encode_ieee
documentation:
Encode Python float to int (32 bits integer) as an IEEE single or double precision format.

Support NaN.

```
param val_float

float value to convert

type val_float

float

param double

set to encode as a 64 bits double precision,
default is 32 bits single (optional)

type double

bool

returns

IEEE 32 bits (single precision) as Python int

rtype

int
```

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.host
documentation:
Get or set the server to connect to.

This can be any string with a valid IPv4 / IPv6 address or hostname.
Setting host to a new value will close the current socket.

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.port
documentation:
Get or set the current TCP port (default is 502).

Setting port to a new value will close the current socket.

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.unit_id
documentation:
Get or set the modbus unit identifier (default is 1).

Any int from 0 to 255 is valid.

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.timeout
documentation:
Get or set requests timeout (default is 30 seconds).

The argument may be a floating point number for sub-second precision.
Setting timeout to a new value will close the current socket.

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.auto_open
documentation: Get or set automatic TCP connect mode (True = turn on).

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.auto_close
documentation: Get or set automatic TCP close after each request mode (True = turn on).

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.is_open
documentation: Get current status of the TCP connection (True = open).

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient.open
documentation:
Connect to modbus server (open TCP connection).

```
returns

connect status (True on success)

rtype

bool
```

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.client.ModbusClient._open
documentation: Connect to modbus server (open TCP connection).

This method is provided to be overridden with user code to catch changes

classes.name: pyModbusTCP.server.ModbusServer

methods.name: pyModbusTCP.server.ModbusServer.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Modbus server constructor.

```
param host

hostname or IPv4/IPv6 address server address (default is 'localhost')

type host

str

param port

TCP port number (default is 502)

type port

int

param no_block

no block mode, i.e. start() will return (default is False)

type no_block

bool

param ipv6

use ipv6 stack (default is False)

type ipv6

bool

param data_bank

instance of custom data bank, if you don't want the default one (optional)

type data_bank

DataBank

param data_hdl

instance of custom data handler, if you don't want the default one (optional)

type data_hdl

DataHandler

param ext_engine

an external engine reference (ref to ext_engine(session_data)) (optional)

type ext_engine

callable

param device_id

instance of DeviceIdentification class for read device identification request (optional)

type device_id

DeviceIdentification
```
